family: Fries-, Photo-Fries, and Anionic Ortho-Fries Rearrangement
rationale: This is the classic Fries rearrangement of phenyl acetate to 2-hydroxyacetophenone, a textbook example. It clearly demonstrates the migration of an acyl group from a phenolic oxygen to an ortho position on the aromatic ring, catalyzed by a Lewis acid (AlCl3). The reactants and products are simple, acyclic where possible, and unambiguously represent the core transformation of this reaction family.
collision_avoidance_note: The structure is kept simple to avoid overlap with other named reactions. Phenyl acetate is a fundamental starting material. The product is a simple hydroxy aryl ketone. This specific transformation is unlikely to be a primary example for other named reactions like Friedel-Crafts acylation (which typically involves an arene and an acyl halide/anhydride) or Claisen rearrangement (which involves allyl phenyl ethers).
